video_title: The Surprising Truth About Who's Buying Bitcoin in Europe

Actually, I had to have this friend of mine who was more a tech nerd who showed me Bitcoin. I had him buy I went home. I was hooked. I tried to buy it, it was too complicated. So, I asked him, "Hey, can you buy me one Bitcoin?" It was 500 bucks back then. So, I gave him basically 500 bucks in cash, and he bought me the Bitcoin because it was too complicated for me as a non-tech nerd guy, right? And it was just too hard. Like nine out of 10 of these people just didn't make it through. They only wanted to buy 100 bucks Bitcoin maybe uh set up a savings plan. That's actually what I wanted to do as well, set up a savings plan for 100 bucks a week or something. And there was nothing that just made it click and make it easy for the 90% of the people that are not tech and finance nerds like many in Bitcoin, right? Uh so that then I ended up really founding this company. I found my uh co-founder who's a CTO who could build these things that I had in mind. And so we went live in summer 2020 just to to make Bitcoin easy to understand and easy to access for people. And ever since we've been on the same mission. Uh still the same. Every day I get up and try to help people understand Bitcoin and and get Bitcoin very easily, but in the right way. Like Bitcoin only, no no uh other uh other other things uh that that make it more complicated in in a self-custodial way. So, what's different about Relay is as well, we're not a typical exchange like Coinbase or in in or Bitpanda in Europe where uh the exchange holds the user fund. We never hold a penny of our users' funds. We're a self-custodial wallet integrated in uh the exchange. >> Why did you choose to do that? >> So, >> [sighs] >> first of all, it's of course an idealistic thing because the full potential and the full benefits of Bitcoin you only get really when you hold Bitcoin yourself. Like take control of your future is our uh mantra basically. We want to help people take control of their money. No counterparty risk. You can send it anywhere in the world to anyone over the internet. No counterparty within a few seconds, and it's the just a true No one can censor it. No one can take it away from you. All of these things only work when when it's um self-custodian people actually hold it. So, we truly believe in that. But also, and that's more the controversial point, is that I believe long-term it's going to be actually easier to use Bitcoin in self-custodial way because it's much less regulated. Now that we have all these licenses, we see what you need to do if you hold Bitcoin on behalf or any asset on behalf of your clients. Um so, of course, now it's still like you need to write down the 12 words, you need to have a hardware wallet, etc. This is for many people like off-putting and too complicated. So, that's why some many of them prefer custodial exchange or even a ETF kind of thing. It's just easier. It's something that people know. But I feel like in the next 5 to 10 years, the industry will come up. And we come are coming up also with solutions like cloud backup, face backup, stuff like that where you actually um don't need to worry about the 12 words at all. You lose your phone, no worries, buy a new phone, download the app, face ID, cloud backup, few clicks, and you have your whole funds and transaction history, and you basically your whole financial uh life back again. And you really hold your coins. And it's actually easier than to uh interact with a custodial kind of traditional financial service, like even a bank. So, I think this will go away, and it will be actually in the self-custodial way it's going to be easier to interact with Bitcoin than in custodial way. There's 100 million people, 8 million people living in, uh, Germany and another eight each in Switzerland and, uh, Austria. Uh, and also we grew pretty quickly then in in France and Italy and Spain, um, a bit less, uh, but still like the long tail in Eastern Europe and Northern Europe. We were not able to actively market in these countries, so this was all organic growth. People just, uh, figured out that there's this Swiss bis- Bitcoin, uh, app that they can use that is super easy and we grew very organically if you uh did the first few years. And all the others 40 plus countries in Europe are in the EU. And when this framework came, basically um if you don't get this license, and we did it get it now, but if you don't get it until end of this month, actually, um you can you need to stop offering completely services uh if you don't have this license in all the EU countries. So, >> What is the license about? >> Uh it's called MiCA license, marketing crypto assets. There's this encompassing EU regulatory framework. Uh it's just you need to get this financial services license, financial intermediary, if you want to offer brokerage services or any financial services in the EU to EU clients. Um so, before with the Swiss license, we could offer our Swiss financial service that was licensed in Switzerland, we could offer it to EU customers. But now, with this whole uh MiCA framework, if you're not licensed in an EU country and have this particular MiCA license, which is quite hard to get, like it's it's almost banking license, that allows you to exchange. Uh it would allow as well for custody, which we don't need. And uh if you do some tweaks, it also allows you for lending and all other different financial service providers. But um uh yeah, this was very hard to get. Fortunately, we got it. So, now we yes, we are active in all EU countries except UK, which again has another regulatory framework. I'm not sure why, but Italy was always like at one point it was the second biggest even uh even surpassing Switzerland. Not sure why. Italy, and then um France now because we have our office and license and do some marketing in in France. And it's also one of the Swiss languages, so we had the French French language from the beginning. Swiss people are very reluctant to use service providers especially in finance like fintech apps from other countries. Maybe in in in Eastern Europe and in Northern Europe, that's a bit the same. What we see for example in Germany, they really like Swiss. They they prefer Swiss brands and and companies to to German ones. So maybe it's just cultural differences. That's the nice thing about Europe, right? Users are coming coming back to the app, they're trading more. It will do much better.
